data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-22T23:08 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-0rlgv [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-22 · Updated: 2026-02-22

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-2319d: Check for orphaned processes ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-mql5q: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-p1jrb: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-t1tk5: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2

content.thinking:
There are multiple completed patrol molecules stacked up. The hooked wisp (skogtown-wisp-wisp-0rlgv) also has all children completed. I should squash the completed patrols and create a fresh one.

Let me check state.json first to see patrol count and whether to loop or hand off.

content.text: Multiple completed patrols stacked. Let me check state and squash.

content.thinking:
Patrol count is 18, which is close to the handoff threshold of 20. No extraordinary action. I should squash the stale patrols and create a new one. After this patrol (19), and the next (20), I'd hand off. Let me squash and create a fresh patrol.

Actually, wait - there are 4 completed patrol molecules plus the hooked wisp with all children done. These need to be cleaned up. Let me squash them and then create a new patrol, incrementing the count to 19.

content.text: Patrol count at 18, no extraordinary actions. Squashing stale patrols and creating fresh cycle.

input.command: gt mol squash --jitter 10s --summary "Patrol 19: routine cycle, all prior patrols complete, no incidents"
